findings.evidence_quote: Los servicios bancarios para las cuentas Credit Karma Money son proporcionados por MVB Bank, Inc., miembro de la FDIC.
findings.location: Offer disclaimer - Credit Karma Money account section
findings.reasoning: Trigger: The page mentions a deposit product (Credit Karma Money checking account, 'cuenta corriente Credit Karma Money') which is a deposit account, triggering R-03's requirement for proper FDIC insurance language. Requirement analysis: The rule requires the language to state 'Deposit product is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through [Bank]'. The actual disclosure here says the banking services are 'provided by MVB Bank, Inc., member of FDIC' - this states FDIC membership but does NOT explicitly state the $250,000 insurance coverage limit language required by the rule. This is the standard Intuit approved disclosure pattern for deposit accounts, but it doesn't literally include the '$250,000' figure. Axis A: since the specific $250,000 language is absent, technically the requirement is not met in the strict sense the rule specifies (state 'FDIC-insured up to $250,000'). Counter-reading considered: One could argue 'miembro de FDIC' broadly implies standard FDIC insurance which is always capped at $250,000 by law, so the intent of consumer protection is satisfied even without the numeric figure - but the rule explicitly requires the numeric cap to be stated. Given the rule's specific wording requirement, the disclosure is deficient because it never states the $250,000 figure anywhere in connection with the deposit product. This constitutes a violation of axis A. Axis B: this is the approved standard library disclosure pattern used across many Intuit deposit-related asset pages (mentioning 'miembro de FDIC' for MVB Bank), so wording matches library entries, axis B true even though it doesn't include the dollar figure - the library entry itself may not require it. Given axis A non-compliant despite Axis B matching, this is approved_but_non_compliant.

findings.evidence_quote: El acceso a tu reembolso de impuestos federales hasta 5 días antes es diferente del depósito electrónico del reembolso de impuestos estándar y depende de que el IRS envíe al banco los detalles del reembolso antes de la fecha de aprobación del pago.
findings.location: 5 days early / Refund deposit disclosure section
findings.reasoning: Trigger: this section discusses the Credit Karma Money deposit account tied to refund deposits, again invoking the deposit product FDIC disclosure requirement. Requirement analysis: nowhere in this refund-early-access disclosure or its surrounding text does the page state the specific '$250,000' FDIC insurance coverage limit for the deposit account tied to this feature; it only references 'FDIC' membership status of MVB Bank elsewhere. The rule requires explicit statement of the $250,000 insured amount for deposit products - this is absent throughout the page for this deposit-linked feature. Axis A: non-compliant since the specific dollar-amount insurance language is missing. Axis B: the wording used elsewhere (MVB Bank, member FDIC) matches Intuit's standard approved disclosure library style for these features (which typically omits the $250k figure at the point of product mention, relying on general 'member FDIC' phrasing), so axis B is true (matches approved wording pattern) despite not fully satisfying the substantive requirement of stating the $250k coverage cap. Strongest counter-reading: one could argue the rule is satisfied by the general reference to 'miembro de la FDIC' since FDIC insurance is inherently capped at $250,000 by regulation and does not need restating on every product mention; under a practical reading, standard 'member FDIC' disclosures are treated as compliant boilerplate across the industry. However, the rule as canonically stated requires explicit mention of the $250,000 figure, which is not present anywhere on this page for the deposit product, so I flag this as non-compliant to be rigorous per the rule's literal text.

findings.evidence_quote: Credit Karma no es un banco. Los servicios bancarios para las cuentas Credit Karma Money son proporcionados por MVB Bank, Inc., miembro de la FDIC.
findings.location: TurboTax Online/mobile offers section – Credit Karma Money disclosure
findings.reasoning:
The rule triggers whenever a deposit product is being advertised. This page promotes Credit Karma Money Spend checking accounts in connection with TurboTax refund options and clearly states that banking services are provided by MVB Bank, Inc., member FDIC. That is a deposit product reference, so trigger_met is true.

Rule R-03 requires that, when a deposit product is advertised, the FDIC language state: "Deposit product is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through Bank". The page only says that MVB Bank is a member of the FDIC and mentions no coverage limit or phrasing equivalent to "FDIC‑insured up to $250,000 through [Bank]." Therefore the requirement is not met (axis A non‑compliant). Because the wording does not match the required canonical language, axis B is also false. The strongest counter-argument would be that merely saying "miembro de la FDIC" might be considered sufficient FDIC disclosure; however, the explicit rule text requires the more specific insurance statement including the $250,000 limit and construction "Deposit product is FDIC‑insured up to $250,000 through Bank", which is absent. Thus this is an unapproved violation, and the verdict is flag.

findings.evidence_quote: * Obtén tu reembolso de impuestos hasta 5 días antes con una cuenta Credit Karma Money™: cuando llegue el momento de presentar tu declaración, solicita que tu reembolso de impuestos se deposite directamente en una cuenta corriente Credit Karma Money™ y podrías recibir tus fondos hasta 5 días antes. Si eliges pagar el costo de preparación de declaración de impuestos con TurboTax usando tu reembolso de impuestos federales o tomar el préstamo del Adelanto de Reembolso de TurboTax, no podrás recibir tu reembolso hasta 5 días antes con Credit Karma. El programa de cinco días antes puede cambiar o suspenderse en cualquier momento. El acceso a tu reembolso de impuestos federales hasta 5 días antes es diferente del depósito electrónico del reembolso de impuestos estándar y depende de que el IRS envíe al banco los detalles del reembolso antes de la fecha de aprobación del pago. Es posible que el IRS no envíe la información sobre el reembolso con anticipación. Se excluyen las declaraciones de impuestos comerciales. Credit Karma no es un banco. Los servicios bancarios para las cuentas Credit Karma Money son proporcionados por MVB Bank, Inc., miembro de la FDIC. Se aplican saldos máximos y límites de transferencia por cuenta.
findings.location: TurboTax Online/Dispositivos móviles section – bullet about Credit Karma Money account
findings.reasoning: Trigger: The page actively promotes using a Credit Karma Money checking account to receive a tax refund up to 5 days early, which is a deposit product, so the rule applies. Requirement: When a deposit product is advertised, the FDIC insurance language must state it is FDIC-insured up to $250,000 through the bank. The quoted text only says the services are provided by MVB Bank, member FDIC; it does not state “FDIC-insured up to $250,000,” so Axis A is not met. Axis B: The wording does not match the approved library entry verbatim or equivalent; it lacks the $250,000 coverage statement, so Axis B is false. Strongest counter-reading considered: One could argue this is merely informational or a partner disclaimer rather than advertising. However, the copy encourages consumers to use/open the account to get an early-refund benefit, which constitutes marketing of a deposit account; thus the rule is triggered and the required FDIC coverage wording is missing.
